title: Well-posedness of the coupled process v-tilde and equivalence of its law to v on infinite horizon

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body:
**Where:** equations `spdehhh` (definition of $\tilde v$) and proof of `secondres` in `L:27`.

**Gap.** The proof never establishes (a) existence/uniqueness of the asymptotically-coupled $\tilde v$ solving the mild equation with feedback drift $\lambda_n(u_r-\tilde v_r)$ and $\mathcal C^{-1}$ nonlinearity $h$; and, crucially for the ergodic argument, (b) that $\mathrm{Law}(\tilde v)\ll\mathrm{Law}(v)$ (mutually a.c.) on path space over $[0,\infty)$. The overview promises (b); the proof jumps from the integrability $\int_0^\infty\!\int_D\lambda_t^2\mathbb E|u_t-\tilde v_t|^2<\infty$ straight to "Pinsker + Girsanov [BKS18, Lemma A.1, Thm A.2]".

**To prove.** Express the feedback drift $g_t=\lambda_t(u_t-\tilde v_t)$ through the noise's Cameron–Martin space; verify the infinite-horizon Girsanov density is a genuine probability change (generalized-coupling / Novikov-type integrability of [BKS18]); and verify the hypotheses of the cited [BKS18] lemmas hold for *this* SPDE.
